preamble: The transformation of the voiced velar fricative [ɣ] to the voiceless uvular plosive [q] is a documented historical and dialectal shift, primarily observed in various Arabic dialects and some Berber languages. This change involves a combination of fortition, devoicing, and backing of the articulation point.
phoneticEffects: Fortition (fricative to plosive), Devoicing (voiced to voiceless), Backing (velar to uvular)

examples.note: General substitution in various phonetic environments, serving as an identity marker.
'Tuareg man' to 'Tuareg woman' (feminine form, involving grammatical gender agreement)

sources: Alsiraih, W. (2021). Speech Variation in the Dialect of Basrah. Journal of the College of Arts, University of Basrah, 96(2).
Boussaid, A. (2018). The Substitution of [gh] for [q] in El-Meni'a Spoken Arabic. ResearchGate.
Taine-Cheikh, C. (2020). Arabic and contact-induced change. Language Science Press.
Heath, J. (2021). A grammar of Tagdal, a Northern Songhay language. LOT Publications.
